items.title: Light made a flip phone. It’s colorful and it’s cheap.
items.summary: Light co-founder Kaiwei Tang helped create the Motorola Razr, and he's as surprised as anyone that over twenty years later, the flip phone is relevant again.

items.title: After TikTok, Snap settles social media addiction case
items.summary: Snap is the latest big tech company to settle a lawsuit that claimed social media platforms are harmful to children and young adults. The company has reached a “tentative” agreement in a c...

items.title: AI music generator Suno breach affects 55M users, per Have I Been Pwned
items.summary: A hacker took names, phone numbers, and physical addresses of millions of customers who used AI music generator Suno.

items.title: What to know about the landmark Warner Bros. Discovery sale
items.summary: Learn more about Paramount's planned acquisition of Warner Bros. Discovery — a historic Hollywood megadeal valued at $111 billion — as it continues to develop.

items.title: UK government scraps plans for digital ID cards after millions of Brits opposed
items.summary: The new U.K. government scrapped a digital ID program that millions of British people opposed, in place of a tax cut to tackle the cost of living.

items.title: The Xteink X4 Pro could be the tiny e-reader of your dreams
items.summary: Xteink makes its pocket-sized e-reader even better with a touchscreen and front light.

items.title: Music streamer Deezer says more than 50% of daily uploads are AI-generated
items.summary: Deezer said more than 90,000 AI-generated tracks were uploaded daily on the platform in June.

items.title: Bluecore Energy raises $10M to build portable nuclear reactors on barges
items.summary: Maritime nuclear energy startup Bluecore Energy has raised $10 million in a pre-seed funding round led by Slauson & Co.

items.title: Gritt exits stealth with $32 million for robots to build solar plants — then, everything else
items.summary: Gritt is coming out of stealth with $34 million and plans to automate the hardest tasks on construction sites.

items.title: Anthropic’s landmark $1.5B copyright settlement is approved
items.summary: The final approval settles one case, but it doesn't resolve the broader issue of using copyrighted works to train AI models.

items.title: Colossal Biosciences reportedly in talks to raise new capital at $20B–$30B valuation
items.summary: The de-extinction startup is looking to double or triple its previous valuation, according to the report.

items.title: Trump’s latest AI czar has already resigned
items.summary: The director role for the Center for AI Standards and Innovation (CAISI) has become a revolving door since David Sacks left his position as czar.

items.title: Google is working on a new AI chip designed to make Gemini more efficient
items.summary: Alphabet, Google's parent company, is reportedly working on a new chip designed to make its Gemini models run much more efficiently.

items.title: AI’s most important protocol is getting a little bit easier to use
items.summary: Under the new system, the protocol will take a looser, "stateless" approach to session IDs on the server side, similar to how most ordinary websites already work.

items.title: X relaunches a rebuilt Android app after year-long effort
items.summary: X says the rebuilt version of its Android app is now available globally.

items.title: OpenAI is scared of open-weight models. Should the US be?
items.summary: Talk of banning Chinese-made open-weight LLMs reveals the challenge of  turning AI into a business.

items.title: Natural raises $30M to reinvent payments for AI agents — and take on Stripe
items.summary: The one-year-old startup aims to reinvent financial architecture for autonomous AI transactions.

items.title: Judge pauses $110B Paramount-Warner Bros. merger
items.summary: The lawsuit from the states alleges that the deal would harm movie theaters, basic cable distributors, and audiences.

items.title: Adobe camera app’s new feature will critique your photos using AI
items.summary: Adobe's Project Indigo can now remove all kinds of backgrounds from photos you snap using the app.

items.title: Hackers are exploiting recently patched WordPress bugs, putting millions of websites at risk
items.summary: Two critical security flaws in WordPress’ software have given hackers the chance to remotely take over tens of millions of websites, according to an estimate by a cybersecurity researcher.
